skeptic.top_risk: Load-bearing parameter set is admittedly anecdotal (~24, unfinalized), so rare-but-visible + spam-unprofitable is asserted not demonstrated.
skeptic.cheapest_evidence: Offline replay of existing channel message logs across low/med/high channels to compute spawn frequency and paced-spam ROI under candidate parameters before shipping any UI.
skeptic.report: Repo's first message-triggered event, reuses services. Failure economic-tuning; both claims reduce to unmeasured numbers; spam-farming via colluding accounts / private channel; item cannibalization (items less re-tunable); rare-but-visible vs spam-unprofitable in tension for single constant. Cheapest evidence: offline log replay + item-value calc. Complexity contained by reuse but first message-triggered surface. Smallest slice: XP-only or low coins, defer items, runtime-configurable params. BUILD conditionally; hold item rewards until (b) measured.

builder.top_risk: Two load-bearing claims both unvalidated — second explicitly unmeasured.
builder.cheapest_evidence: Replay historical per-channel message logs through debounce+threshold logic offline to measure spawn cadence across low/med/high traffic before writing any live spawn code.
builder.report: Core is engagement engine; novel piece is trigger surface. Claim (a) is a tension not a setting — fixed count behaves differently 10-msg/day vs 10-msg/min; may need per-channel/adaptive calibration = more complexity than MVP implies. Claim (b) substitution risk not measured. Secondary claim-race concurrency not measured. Cheapest evidence: offline replay + farm sim + earn baseline + concurrency load test. Reuse caps blast radius but trigger/claim half new+race-prone. Smallest slice: offline replay harness (kill gate), then one guild coins-only no items, add items after telemetry.

user_value.top_risk: Reward-parameter set unvalidated: may fail rare-but-visible across tiers, or quietly cannibalize fishing/mining earn rates (self-flagged unmeasured).
user_value.cheapest_evidence: Replay historical per-channel message logs through debounce+threshold logic offline for spawn-frequency-per-tier and projected reward payout vs current fishing/mining earn rates — no shipping.
user_value.report: Passive engagement engine, mints no new currency (strongest part). Failure: parameter squeeze — loose=spam/farmable, tight=invisible; single ~24 constant can't satisfy tiers. Second: cannibalization, players shift to free spawns, unmeasured. Cheapest evidence: offline replay + arithmetic, pre-launch earn baseline. Cost low; novel risk is first-valid-claimer race. Smallest slice: one/few pilot channels single tier, hardcoded params, coins-and-XP only items deferred, metrics from first commit. BUILD gated.

synthesizer.top_risk: Both load-bearing claims are unvalidated numbers: a single ~24 message-count constant is structurally squeezed between rare-but-visible and spam-unprofitable across traffic tiers, and reward draw from fishing/mining may quietly cannibalize those earn rates (self-flagged, not measured).
synthesizer.cheapest_evidence: Replay historical per-channel message logs through the debounce+threshold logic offline to measure spawn cadence per traffic tier and projected reward payout vs current fishing/mining earn rates — no live code, no UI shipped.
synthesizer.report: Panel synthesis (3/3 agree BUILD @ 0.6); same kill-gate evidence and smallest slice. Core claim: passive engagement engine, mints no new currency; novel surface narrow (message trigger + first-valid-claimer race). Strongest failure: economic parameter squeeze — fixed message-count threshold behaves differently 10 msgs/day vs 10 msgs/min; single global ~24 constant structurally cannot satisfy rare-but-visible AND spam-unprofitable across tiers, likely forces per-channel/adaptive calibration; reward cannibalization self-flagged unmeasured (items less re-tunable than coins); concurrency correctness on Claim race not measured; secondary colluding accounts / private channels. Cheapest evidence: offline replay of existing logs + item-value injection calc vs pre-launch earn baseline + concurrency load test; kill gate before UI. Cost low via reuse but trigger/claim half new+race-prone, first message-triggered surface. Smallest slice: offline replay/farm-sim/earn-baseline harness first (kill gate), then one guild / few channels single tier, hardcoded-but-runtime-configurable params, coins+XP only defer items until (b) measured, metrics from first commit.
